zotero.abstractNote: The method of lines (MOL) is a standard approach for solving time-dependent partial differential equations (PDEs) by discretizing the spatial domain to transform the PDE into a system of ordinary differential equations (ODEs). In this work, we propose a probabilistic numerical method of lines (PN-MOL). By replacing the deterministic ODE solver with a probabilistic one, we obtain a posterior distribution over the solution of the PDE that quantifies the uncertainty arising from the temporal discretization. We demonstrate that the PN-MOL is computationally efficient, provides well-calibrated uncertainty estimates, and allows for the integration of prior knowledge about the solution.
zotero.conferenceName: 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics

extra.Editors: Camps-Valls, Gustau and Ruiz, Francisco J. R. and Valera, Isabel

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
